channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising Environmental Impact of AI Datacentres and Ways to Reduce Energy Use
item.description: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence has led to a surge in datacentres, causing significant environmental strain globally and in Australia. Datacentre electricity demand is growing at four t...

item.title: Government Plans Support Measures for Exporters Amid West Asia Conflict
item.description: The commerce department of India is actively preparing support measures to assist exporters in coping with the impact of the ongoing West Asia conflict. The government has prioritised the shipment of ...

item.title: Rising Diesel Prices Strain Filipino Jeepney and Tricycle Drivers’ Livelihoods
item.description: Diesel prices in the Philippines are climbing sharply, fueled by ongoing conflict in the Middle East, and this is severely affecting public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers. Jeepney driver Renie Rabago, ...

item.title: Matildas advance to Asian Cup semi-final despite tough North Korea test
item.description: The Matildas secured a hard-fought 2-1 win over North Korea in the quarter-finals of the 2026 Women’s Asian Cup, keeping their hopes of continental glory alive. Despite being dominated for much of the...

item.title: Court Case Filed Over Disappearance of Nairobi Businessman Abdi Ali
item.description: The Kenyan government is facing a lawsuit following the alleged abduction of businessman Abdi Ali in Eastleigh, Nairobi. According to lawyer Mwaura Wakabata of Alakonya & Associates, Ali was reportedl...

item.title: NNL honours top players, coaches, and teams for December to February 2026
item.description: The Nigeria National League (NNL) don give honour to those wey perform well for their monthly awards covering the end of 2025 and early 2026. For December 2025, Ajibola Joel of Stormers SC score the h...

item.title: Africa Needs Urgent Water Security Amid Crop Failures from Failed Rains
item.description: Africa faces a big challenge as rains fail time and again, leading to poor harvests and hunger in many areas. Farmers depend on rains for their crops, but with climate change messing things up, drough...

item.title: US Grants 30-Day Waiver on Russian Oil Sanctions Amid Soaring Prices from Iran Conflict
item.description: The United States has given a 30-day temporary waiver allowing countries to purchase sanctioned Russian oil and petroleum products that are currently at sea. This move aims to calm global oil and gas ...

item.title: FCC Orders Fresh Recruitment of Class-IV Staff at Karak DHQ Hospital Following Irregularities
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) has expressed serious concern over irregularities in public appointments at the District Headquarters Hospital in Karak, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. The two-j...

item.title: Potential Regional Tensions Arising from Kurdish Involvement in the Iran Conflict
item.description: Since the US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran began, the nature of Donald Trump’s war objectives has shifted between targeting Iran’s military and attempting to topple its regime. Analysts suggest that ...

item.title: Deputy President Mashatile Announces South Africa's Introduction of Six-Monthly HIV Prevention Injection
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has welcomed South Africa's planned rollout of Lenacapavir, a long-acting HIV prevention injection, describing it as a potential turning point in the country’s fight ag...

item.title: Oscars 2026 Security Heightened with One-Mile Perimeter Due to Ongoing U.S.-Iran War and General Precautions
item.description: Security for the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026, at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood has been significantly reinforced, surpassing measures seen in previous years, including those during the 2003...

item.title: The Strategic Vulnerability of Gulf Cities Due to Dependence on Desalination
item.description: The Gulf region, long defined by its oil wealth, is increasingly vulnerable due to its reliance on desalinated water. Major urban centres such as Dubai, Doha, Kuwait City, and Abu Dhabi depend heavily...

item.title: Baguio experiences chilly weather with morning temperature at 9°C
item.description: Baguio City recorded a low temperature of 9 degrees Celsius at around 5 a.m. on Saturday, March 14, 2026, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration ...

item.title: PDEA arrests two shabu dealers near school campuses in Central Mindanao
item.description: Two men were arrested for selling shabu near schools in Central Mindanao on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, in separate entrapment operations conducted by the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) Regi...

item.title: Special Non-Working Holidays Set in Three Philippine Cities for Local Anniversaries
item.description: The Philippine government has officially declared special non-working holidays in three cities this March to mark local founding and charter anniversaries. In Negros Occidental, the cities of Sipalay ...

item.title: Labour’s Shifting Support Among Ethnic Minority Voters
item.description: Historically, Labour has enjoyed strong support from ethnic minority communities in the UK, particularly Black and Asian voters. However, recent trends suggest that this loyalty may be waning. Indian-...

item.title: Bill Shorten Proposes Business Tax to Support University Funding Amid Student Debt Concerns
item.description: Bill Shorten, speaking in his capacity as vice-chancellor of the University of Canberra, has highlighted the growing challenges in funding Australian universities. In his 2026 UC Aitkin Lecture at the...

item.title: WPL Clubs Request Weekend Matches Be Postponed Due to Venue Challenges
item.description: Kenya's Women's Premier League (WPL) clubs have formally requested the Football Kenya Federation (FKF) to push all matches scheduled for this weekend, March 14-15, to the following week. The request c...

item.title: Iran calls for justice and lasting peace for Palestinians on Quds Day
item.description: For Quds Day wey dey happen every last Friday of Ramadan, Iran don call for justice and lasting peace for the Palestinian people. Iran Ambassador to Nigeria, Mahdavi Raja, talk this one for Abuja duri...
